pattern: Because every value sits in the range 1..n, the array can index itself: flip the sign at the slot a value points to, and a slot already negative reveals a second sighting of that value, giving constant extra space.
hints: The values are confined to 1..n, so each one names a position in the very same array you could use as a scratch marker.
Visit each value v and toggle a marker (for instance, negate) at index v-1; if that marker is already set, v has been seen before.
Iterate once: for each element take its absolute value a, look at index a-1, and if nums[a-1] is already negative record a, otherwise negate nums[a-1].

edge_inputs.description: mixed array with two duplicated values
every value distinct yields no duplicates
all elements are the same value
duplicates spread to the array ends
the largest value n is itself duplicated

stress.description: permutation of 1..n with a random subset of positions duplicated by overwriting others, keeping every value within 1..n
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    dup_count = rng.randint(0, n // 2)
    chosen = rng.sample(range(n), min(dup_count, n)) if n > 1 else []
    for idx in chosen:
        v = nums[idx]
        target = rng.randrange(n)
        if target != idx:
            nums[target] = v
    return [nums]
stress.note: overwriting some slots with an existing value makes duplicates while every entry stays in 1..n
